c_signature: MPFR_COLD_FUNCTION_ATTR void mpfr_assert_fail (const char *filename, int linenum, const char *expr)
doc:
PARKED. C runtime assertion handler -- prints diagnostic + calls abort(). The TS port uses native `throw new Error(...)` for invariant violations (CLAUDE.md Rule 1: fail fast, fail loud) and MPFRError for documented domain errors. There is no equivalent function to port and no public-API consumer would invoke it.

Not ADR 0002 criterion specifically -- this is a runtime-system function that has no semantic equivalent in idiomatic TS. Similar to mpfr_allocate_func / mpfr_free_func / mpfr_reallocate_func (parked in the same vein during earlier session).
divergence_from_c: PARKED -- not ported. TS uses throw + MPFRError.
refs: src/core.ts -- MPFRError, the TS equivalent for domain-error throws.
